cards.back: A paging algorithm where pages are fetched into memory only when needed, reducing memory usage and improving system efficiency.
A technique used to reduce the overhead of updating pages in virtual memory, where a new copy of a page is created when the original is modified.

cards.front: What is the purpose of page replacement algorithms?
cards.back: To determine which page to replace when the page fault threshold is reached, ensuring efficient use of memory and minimizing the number of page faults.

cards.front: What is the difference between private and shared pages?
cards.back: Private pages are reserved for a single process and are not shared with other processes, while shared pages are shared between multiple processes, reducing memory usage.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the page table?
cards.back: To map virtual addresses to physical addresses, allowing the operating system to manage memory allocation and deallocation efficiently.
